problem.title: Super Ugly Number
problem.description: A super ugly number is a positive integer whose prime factors are in the given array primes. Given an integer n and an array of primes, return the nth super ugly number. The 1st super ugly number is 1.
assumptions: primes is sorted in ascending order.
Generalization of Ugly Number II to arbitrary prime factors.

example.explanation: Sequence: [1,2,4,7,8,13,14,16,19,26,28,32]. Generalize the three-pointer DP: maintain one pointer per prime. Next = min over all primes of dp[ptr[i]] * primes[i]. Advance all pointers that produced the minimum.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – DP with k Pointers [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Generalize Ugly Number II to k primes. Maintain an array of pointers (one per prime) into the dp array. At each step compute next = min over all i of dp[ptr[i]] * primes[i]. Set dp[j] = next, then advance every pointer whose product equals next (to avoid duplicates).
